papers.title: Decomposition in Fe–Mn–Al–C lightweight steels
papers.paper_scope: Study of austenite decomposition reactions at 600°C and 800°C for different chemical compositions in Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels, characterizing discontinuous precipitation, precipitation transformation, cellular transformation, and spinodal decomposition using optical microscopy, SEM, EDS, and Vickers hardness.

processes.description: Hot-rolled at 1150°C obtaining 5 mm flat-products, followed by water quenching.
processes.processes_notes: Common initial thermomechanical processing for all cast ingots.

processes.description: Tempered at 600°C for 30 min, followed by water quenching.
processes.processes_notes: Triggered discontinuous precipitation and precipitation transformation.

processes.description: Tempered at 800°C for 30 min, followed by water quenching.
processes.processes_notes: Triggered cellular transformation and/or spinodal decomposition.

structures.overall_structure: Partial discontinuous precipitation into γ + κ lamellar structures starting from grain boundaries; partial transformation only

phases_present.morphology: parent grains with partial lamellar transformation

structures.overall_structure: Almost fully transformed austenite via discontinuous precipitation into γ + κ fine lamellae; few γ-band-shaped regions survived; possible nano-sized κ-carbide precipitation in non-transformed austenite

precipitates.distribution: fine lamellae and possible intragranular nano-precipitates

structures.overall_structure: Cellular transformation producing α + κ + residual γ lamellar structures; spinodal decomposition in γ + nano-sized κ-carbides; thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains

precipitates.distribution: intragranular within spinodally decomposed γ; lamellar in cellular transformed regions

structures.overall_structure: Precipitation transformation featuring fine/coarse γ + κ lamellae, carbon-depleted γ bands, and globular κ-carbides; not ordered discontinuous precipitation

phases_present.morphology: bands with carbon-depleted regions and globular precipitates

precipitates.distribution: within carbon-depleted γ bands and along δ/γ boundaries

structures.overall_structure: Complete cellular transformation into α + κ coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing; scattered thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains

phases_present.morphology: coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing

structures.overall_structure: Full austenitic decomposition via cellular transformation into α + κ + residual γ lamellae along with spinodal decomposition; wide and thick lath-like κ-carbide lamellae; δ-ferrite branches inside spinodal decomposed γ grains

phases_present.morphology: residual lamellae and spinodally decomposed grains
branches extending into spinodal decomposed γ grains
wide and thick lath-like lamellae; cuboidal nano-sized precipitates

precipitates.distribution: cuboidal nano-precipitates in spinodal regions; thick lath-like lamellae in cellular regions

structures.overall_structure: Fully spinodal decomposed austenite with nano-sized κ-carbides; acicular-like precipitates in finest and less dense configuration

precipitates.distribution: intragranular acicular-like precipitates, finest and less dense configuration

structures.overall_structure: Parallel striations with different directions in each austenite grain; secondary phases along grain boundaries (probably δ + α); no detected austenite transformation
Parallel striations with different directions in each austenite grain; κ-carbides along grain boundaries; no detected austenite transformation

interfaces.phase_evolution: Discontinuous precipitation initiated at γ grain boundaries and grew towards adjacent γ grains, forming intergranular lamellar structures.
Strong κ-carbide precipitation along δ/γ boundaries and poor precipitation within ferrite; carbon-depleted γ bands with globular κ-carbides.
δ-ferrite branches extend inside spinodal decomposed γ grains; cellular transformation produces α + κ lamellae.

characterization_methods.key_findings: Partial to almost complete γ + κ lamellar structures starting from grain boundaries; few γ-band-shaped regions survived in 9Al-10Mn-1C.
Fine γ + κ-carbide lamellae produced by discontinuous precipitation; possible nano-sized κ-carbide precipitation in non-discontinuous precipitated austenite.
In 9Al-10Mn-1C tempered at 600°C, measured Al and Mn wt% ranges: δ (Al 7.8-8.7, Mn 9.7-10.0), γ (Al 7.6-8.0, Mn 9.9-10.7), κ (Al 8.0-8.3, Mn 10.2-10.5), γ nano-κ (Al 7.8-8.1, Mn 13.2-13.6). Table 3.
Random-like transformation with fine/coarse γ + κ lamellae, carbon-depleted γ bands, and globular κ-carbides; not ordered discontinuous precipitation. Fig. 4.

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure observation of cellular transformation and spinodal decomposition
characterization_methods.key_findings: Cellular transformation produced α + κ + residual γ lamellar structures; spinodal decomposition generated γ + nano-sized κ-carbides; thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains present. Figs. 5(a), 6.
In 9Al-10Mn-1C tempered at 800°C, measured Al and Mn wt% ranges: δ (Al 8.1-8.7, Mn 7.4-9.1), γ (Al 6.8-8.7, Mn 11.1-12.3), κ (Al 8.6-10.0, Mn 10.1-13.8). Table 6.
Complete cellular transformation into α + κ coarse lamellae with wide inter-lamellar spacing; scattered thin band-shaped δ-ferrite grains. Fig. 5(b).

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure observation of cellular transformation and spinodal decomposition
characterization_methods.key_findings: Full austenitic decomposition via cellular transformation into α + κ + residual γ lamellae along with spinodal decomposition; wide and thick lath-like κ-carbide lamellae. Figs. 5(c), 7.
In 12Al-20Mn-1C tempered at 800°C, measured Al and Mn wt% ranges: δ (Al 11.0-13.6, Mn 15.2-20.8), γ (Al 5.7-9.9, Mn 20.3-27.6), κ (Al 10.3-11.9, Mn 21.9-25.5). Table 7.

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase morphology analysis of spinodal decomposition
characterization_methods.key_findings: Fully spinodal decomposed austenite with nano-sized acicular-like κ-carbides in finest and less dense configuration. Fig. 5(d), Fig. 8(e,f).

characterization_methods.purpose: Bulk hardness measurement across alloys and conditions
characterization_methods.key_findings: 5 kg load, 5 indentations per sample; outliers excluded via Minitab18. Hardness values reported for hot-rolled, 600°C tempered, and 800°C tempered conditions. Tables 2, 4, 5, 8, 9.

unmapped_findings: Phase volume fraction diagrams (Figs. 9 and 12) illustrate thermodynamic gradients for austenite stability versus temperature, but the computational method, software, or thermodynamic database used to generate them is not specified in the text.

9Al-20Mn-1C and 9Al-30Mn-1C tempered at 600°C exhibited hardening (359 HV and 330 HV respectively) without detected austenite transformation; solid solution strengthening of carbon and/or very fine nano κ-carbides are hypothesized (Table 8, Fig. 10).
Parallel striations with different directions in each austenite grain were observed in 9Al-20Mn-1C and 9Al-30Mn-1C at 600°C (Fig. 10), requiring further investigation to understand their nature.
Inter-lamellar spacing was characterized qualitatively as narrow for 9Al-10Mn-1C at 600°C and wide for 12Al-10Mn-1C at 800°C, correlating with hardening response (Table 9, Fig. 2 vs Fig. 5).
Figure 14 shows δ-ferrite branches extending inside spinodal decomposed γ grains in 12Al-20Mn-1C at 800°C, a phenomenon requiring further studies to be properly understood.

The best microstructure for combined hardness and expected tensile ductility is suggested to be 9Al-10Mn-1C at 600°C, where carbides obtained by discontinuous precipitation have narrow inter-lamellar spacing and the highest hardness value (439 HV).
